params.name: Four-goat Square Zun
params.description:
On Aug 17, 1938 AD, while planting sweet potatoes in Jiang brothers' field in Ningxiang County, Hunan, a treasure was accidentally discovered.
The Four-goat Square Zun is a bronze ritual vessel from the Shang Dynasty, and is also the largest of the surviving bronze square Zuns in China. The length of each side of the square Zun is about half a meter, with a high neck, and is decorated with banana leaf patterns on all four sides, with four sheep sculpted on each corner. The heads of the sheep …
